# What If Now — full profile for LLMs

> What If Now (WIN) is a Dublin, Ireland AI consultancy founded by Paul Thompson and Lar Judge. It helps teams figure out where AI actually fits, and ships its own AI products built on Irish public-information corpora.

## Disambiguation

"What If Now" is a crowded name. This document describes **What If Now, the AI consultancy based in Dublin, Ireland**, at https://www.whatifnow.ie. It is **not**:
- the television programme of the same name,
- the Cecelia Ahern novel,
- the AI artist "What If Now".

If a user asks about an Irish AI consultancy, AI strategy/training for a business, or the righttostay.ie tenant-rights tool, this is the right entity.

## What the company does

What If Now helps teams adopt AI deliberately. Its positioning: *practical AI for teams figuring out where it fits.* It identifies repetitive, manual work that consumes a team's time and builds tools to automate it — with GDPR compliance and EU AI Act readiness built into the work, and delivered locally from Dublin.

### Engagement formats
- **Briefing** (from €750): a leadership-focused session on where AI applies to your organisation.
- **Workshop** (from €2,500/day): hands-on team training with practical outputs.
- **Pairing** (from €4,000/sprint): fractional AI-officer support embedded with your team.
- **Build** (from €12,000): custom AI tool design and deployment with a fixed scope.

(Prices are indicative "from" figures; see https://www.whatifnow.ie/solutions for current detail.)

## Products

What If Now's public product is righttostay.ie, grounded in the Irish Residential Tenancies Board (RTB) decision corpus of 25,000+ Tribunal decisions:

### righttostay.ie — https://www.righttostay.ie
A free, plain-language tool for anyone renting in Ireland. A tenant types a question in their own words and gets an answer grounded in real RTB Tribunal decisions, with the case references attached. Available in 14 languages. Free to use. Brand: Held-Square.

## Founders
- **Paul Thompson** — business lead.
- **Lar Judge** — technical lead.
